dimensions.description: Does it reproduce published pooled estimates / flow counts within tolerance on a committed gold set, with a deterministic auditable engine? (MEASURED, not self-asserted.)
dimensions.label: Measured output correctness (gold-standard reproduction)

dimensions.description: Does it verify calculations, statistical claims, numerical baselines, or meta-analytic outputs?
dimensions.label: Statistical and numerical verification

dimensions.description: Does it automate study screening, coding, extraction, or related review labor?
dimensions.label: Screening and data-extraction automation

dimensions.description: Are outputs tied to papers, citations, quotes, source records, or auditable evidence?
dimensions.label: Evidence and citation grounding

dimensions.description: Can the system retrieve papers from a large scholarly corpus or index?
dimensions.label: Scholarly search and corpus access

dimensions.description: Does it cover search, screening, extraction, reporting, or living-review workflow steps?
dimensions.label: Systematic-review workflow coverage

dimensions.description: Can it repeatedly monitor, route, verify, or gate work across projects?
dimensions.label: Continuous verification and orchestration

dimensions.description: Does it preserve audit trails, block unsafe outputs, or enforce transparent governance?
dimensions.label: Auditability and fail-closed governance

dimensions.description: Does public documentation expose validation, benchmark, simulation, or test evidence?
dimensions.label: Public benchmark transparency

dimensions.description: Can the user inspect, modify, self-host, or run the system locally?
dimensions.label: Local/open control

interpretation: Use this as a capability and governance benchmark, not as an accuracy leaderboard.
External systems are scored from public documentation and publications; they were not run on shared fixtures here.
A true accuracy benchmark requires a locked task set, blinded expected answers, and the same inputs submitted to each system.
methodology: Dimension weights rebalanced 2026-06-04 so output-correctness + synthesis dominate governance/infra (now ~29% of weight, was ~45%). output_correctness is MEASURED via gold_benchmark (published pooled-estimate reproduction within tolerance), fail-closed. Comparator scores remain capability-PRESENCE from public documentation (some figures literature-grounded, e.g. Elicit data-extraction ~21% exact match vs humans, Cochrane ESM 10.1002/cesm.70033); they are NOT a hands-on head-to-head, so the ranking measures capability coverage, not a measured output-quality contest. Comparators score 0 on output_correctness because a pooled meta-analytic estimate is not their output product (they assist discovery/screening; the human/RevMan pools) and none publishes a gold reproduction — it reflects scope, NOT a measured failure of a test they were given.

score_scale.0: no public evidence or not a core capability

systems.name: Overmind + Sentinel + TruthCert
notes: This system is strongest as a verifier/governance/orchestration layer.
It is not scored as a literature search corpus unless a search backend is explicitly measured.
output_correctness and statistical_verification are MEASURED (gold_benchmark reproduces published pooled estimates within tolerance); other dimensions are capability-presence.
Comparator scores are capability-presence from public docs (some literature-grounded), NOT a hands-on head-to-head — do not read the ranking as a measured output-quality contest.

score_evidence.auditability_governance: Sentinel, TruthCert, and policy guards provide fail-closed local governance.
score_evidence.citation_grounding: citation_grounding artifact: 2/2 claims resolved to corpus records (ratio=1.0); ungrounded claims are reported, never assumed.
score_evidence.continuous_verification: 3 available runners; meta-verify verdict is not_run.
score_evidence.local_control: System is local, editable Python source in the user's workspace.
score_evidence.output_correctness: gold-standard benchmark: 43/43 fixtures pass; worst pooled logRR deviation 0.0029176432525671453 (MEASURED against published references, fail-closed).
score_evidence.public_benchmarking: 17 projects carry oracle-benchmark signals; meta-verify is not_run.
score_evidence.review_workflow: PRISMA 2020 flow artifact present: 10 identified -> 8 included; 253 indexed evidence-synthesis projects.
score_evidence.screening_extraction: screening artifact: True (10 proposals, 0 auto-included); extraction artifact: True (2 validated, 0 flagged).
score_evidence.search_corpus: corpus_search artifact: provider=offline-jsonl, corpus_size=10, hits=10 (offline retrieval; BM25 lexical re-rank, not semantic).
score_evidence.statistical_verification: 294 indexed projects carry advanced mathematical/statistical signals.

systems.source_summary: Computed from indexed project records and optional current verifier status.

systems.name: DistillerSR
notes: Strong enterprise comparator for regulated literature-review operations.
Statistical verification is scored as limited because public claims focus on workflow and extraction.
systems.source_summary: Public product page describes AI-enabled evidence synthesis, screening rerank, AI classifiers, smart extraction, traceability, audit logs, and PRISMA reporting.

systems.category: AI scientific research and systematic-review assistant
systems.name: Elicit
notes: Strongest comparator for front-door literature search and systematic-review workflow.
Benchmark here does not independently verify Elicit's posted accuracy claims.
systems.source_summary: Public product page describes scholarly search, systematic-review screening and data extraction, sentence-level citations, and posted systematic-review validation.

systems.name: ASReview
notes: Best open-source comparator for active-learning title/abstract screening.
Not scored as a full evidence-synthesis verifier because its core workflow is screening.
systems.source_summary: Public project page describes open-source AI screening, workload reduction, simulation/performance comparison, local control, and transparent systematic reviews.

systems.category: AI academic search and synthesis engine
systems.name: Consensus
notes: Strong comparator for paper-grounded question answering and synthesis.
Not a dedicated systematic-review verification or numerical audit system.
systems.source_summary: Help center describes a large peer-reviewed paper database, hybrid semantic/BM25 search, paper-grounded answers, Pro Analysis, Ask Paper, and Consensus Meter.

systems.category: AI literature search with citation-context classification
systems.name: Scite
notes: Strong comparator for citation-context and claim-support analysis.
Not a systematic-review execution or meta-analysis verification engine.
systems.source_summary: Public page describes full-text search, Smart Citations, evidence-grounded answers, and citation contexts classified as supporting or contradicting.

systems.name: RobotReviewer
notes: Closest historical comparator for risk-of-bias and evidence-synthesis automation.
Scored as research-system evidence, not a currently validated commercial workflow.
systems.source_summary: ACL system-demonstration paper documents RobotReviewer as a biomedical evidence-synthesis automation system.

systems.category: AI scholarly search and paper-understanding platform
systems.name: Semantic Scholar
notes: Strong comparator for scholarly discovery infrastructure.
Scored lower on review execution because it is not a dedicated review workflow tool.
systems.source_summary: Public product page describes automatically generated paper TLDRs and API access for summaries over a large scholarly graph.
